text: Context-cost premise is unmeasured and unfalsifiable as stated; existing per-tool telemetry (memory_status, db.ts call/error/size/duration) was overlooked and is the right first measurement, not a refactor.

Session-continuity merge (orient/resume/handoff/narrative) directly contradicts a recorded design decision: docs/phase-2-engineering-plan.md explicitly says do not overload memory_orient with targeted resume behavior. Fails the draft's own 'different computations' test.
The 22-tool surface was deliberately planned and shipped (CHANGELOG, README, phase plans 2-4, Munin logs), not accidental accretion. The 'sprawl' framing is wrong; the real defect is doc drift.
CLAUDE.md actually has 11 tool rows not 12, plus a stale 'All 12 MCP tools enforce access rules' line. Documentation lacks an enforced single source of truth vs TOOL_DEFINITIONS.

Param-mode consolidation is not capability-preserving: per-tool authorization/redaction paths and per-tool librarian/access coverage tests mean a mode enum collapses multiple safety contracts behind one schema.

memory_update_status mislabeled as 'status sugar'; it is a server-enforced structured write path with canonical sections and compare-and-swap. Second factual error in the original analysis (first was memory_status).

Consolidation would silently degrade selection and blind per-tool telemetry (keyed by tool_name) exactly when evidence is needed; retaining aliases to preserve telemetry negates the context saving (catch-22).
Migration cost understated: dedicated per-tool test suites and per-tool access/librarian coverage make it more than a mechanical rename; reversibility is asymmetric.
Over-concession: 'architecture migration is abandoned' if read as permanent is too strong. Correct conclusion is 'do not consolidate now'; keep surface-curation alive behind measurement.

The doc-drift fix can itself become over-engineering. Generating prose from TOOL_DEFINITIONS (internal constant, template-literal descriptions) is a rabbit hole; correct fix is hand-fix the table + a CI name-consistency test, not a prose generator.

read_batch->read is not behavior-identical today: memory_read adds synthesis freshness metadata and logs opened_result outcomes; memory_read_batch does neither per-result. A merge must decide preserve/fix/accept-change. Safer first step is an internal shared readOne helper, keeping public tools stable.
